When to Use It: When conclusion follows naturally from assumption
Example Skeleton: Assume n even → n=2k → compute → n² even

When to Use It: When direct proof is messy; reverse direction easier
Example Skeleton: Assume n odd → n=2k+1 → n² odd → so if n² even then n even

When to Use It: When assuming the opposite leads to an impossibility
Example Skeleton: Assume smallest positive rational r exists → r/2 < r

What You Try to Show: Find one example where it fails
